selection_policy: Use a native round with TinyStories training evidence as the current trainer backend mix. For each exact objective row, compare the optional-stack round against that native trainer row and publish the faster current observed row as the project-wide fastest selection, including Torch only where it still beats the current native evidence. Optional rows remain operator/reference evidence, or rejected trainer-callable microbench wins, unless a refreshed integration exposes a trainer call path and passes correctness plus TinyStories smoke gates. Every selected optional row without a trainer call path must have a matching inactive promotion decision before this artifact can be generated.
